Refactor cDac tests to use typed views (#126466)

Two goals here:
1. Make it easier to understand what tests are doing by reducing pointer
arithmetic on mock memory fragments
2. Segregate the runtime type mocks in preparation for future usage
outside these immediate unit tests

Mostly this consisted of creating the new Typed views so we can operate
on the memory fragments using named properties rather than offset math.
This change should be following similar patterns that we already looked
at in the initial PR #126025, just scaled up to the entire set of tests.
No product code was changed, all the tests still pass, copilot believes
all test validation has been preserved, and everything looked reasonable
enough to me. There is certainly more refactoring that could be done to
continue refining but I think its preferable to capture progress so far
rather than making more adjustments in an already very large PR.

Fix Cloudflare timeout with fire-and-poll pattern

All POST endpoints now return immediately with a task_id. The handler
runs in a background thread and stores its result in a server-side
_tasks dict. The frontend polls GET /api/task/{id} every 3s until the
task completes.

Also fixes api() JS function to check resp.ok before parsing JSON,
preventing the 'Unexpected token <' error on HTML error pages.

* Fix reverb tail truncation — core reverse-reverb effect was broken (rverse-0d0) (#2)

* Fix reverb tail truncation — pad source with silence so reverb rings out (bd-rverse-0d0)

The reverb output buffer was the same size as the input, so the Schroeder
reverb tail was completely truncated. For short hits (e.g. 50ms kick),
the stretcher had almost nothing to work with.

Fix: Append kReverbTailSeconds (5s) of silence to the source before
reverb processing, then trim trailing silence below kSilenceThreshold
(-60 dB) after. This lets the comb/allpass filters decay naturally,
producing the characteristic reverse-reverb whoosh.

New utilities:
- trimTrailingSilence / trimTrailingSilenceStereo in BufferUtils.h
- kReverbTailSeconds (5.0) and kSilenceThreshold (0.001) in Constants.h

* Fix default params broken by old-format cached state (rverse-7dr)

When PLUG_DOES_STATE_CHUNKS changed from 0 to 1, hosts with cached
old-format state (just serialized params) would feed that into our new
UnserializeState, which read the first 4 bytes as a version number,
advancing startPos. UnserializeParams then started at offset 4 instead
of 0, reading all param values at wrong positions.

Fix: add a magic number (0x52565253 = 'RVRS') as the first field in
our chunk format. UnserializeState now checks for the magic first —
if it doesn't match, falls back to UnserializeParams(chunk, 0) for
backward-compatible old-format restoration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert magic number — use simple version-only chunk format (rverse-7dr)

The magic number broke restore for projects saved with the first
commit's format (version + path + params). The magic check read the
saved version=1 as a non-matching magic, falling back to raw params
and skipping the sample path entirely.

Drop backward compat with pre-persistence state. Chunk format is now
simply: version(int) + path(str) + params. Projects saved before
persistence was added will need to re-save.

Move stretch implementation from header-only (inline) to TimeStretch.cpp
so we can apply per-file -O2 even in Debug configuration. Spectral FFT

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make stretch-only rebuilds synchronous for offline render (bd-g4j)

During offline/bounce rendering, the DAW processes blocks faster than
real-time. The previous async-only stretch path couldn't keep up —
parameter automation changes to riser length were ignored because the
background thread finished after the DAW had already moved past the
relevant blocks.

Fix: setRiserLength() and setBPM() now call rebuildStretch() which
performs the time-stretch synchronously on the calling thread when
cached reversed buffers are available. Falls back to async for the
initial load when no cached buffers exist yet.

This is safe because stretch with -O2 + presetCheaper completes in
~10-50ms, and during offline rendering real-time deadlines don't apply.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use sync stretch only in offline mode via GetRenderingOffline() (bd-g4j)

The previous commit made stretch-only rebuilds always synchronous, which
blocked the audio thread during real-time playback causing severe stutter.

Fix: iPlug2's GetRenderingOffline() detects bounce/export mode. Stretch
rebuilds are now:
- Synchronous during offline rendering (parameter changes picked up immediately)
- Asynchronous during real-time playback (audio thread never blocked)

* Fix Windows resource embedding: add missing bold font and logo to main.rc (bd-rverse-udq) (#16)

On Windows, iPlug2 embeds resources (fonts, images) into the binary
via the Win32 resource compiler (.rc file). main.rc was only embedding
Roboto-Regular.ttf — missing Roboto-Bold.ttf and logo.png.

This caused assertion failures ("No font found") and crashes
("Bitmap not found") when opening the plugin UI on Windows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add main.rc to VST3/CLAP targets on Windows for resource embedding (bd-rverse-th4) (#17)

iPlug2's CMake only adds main.rc (Win32 resource compiler) to the APP
target. Plugin MODULE targets (VST3, CLAP) are missing it, so fonts
and images don't get embedded — causing "No font found" assertion and
crash when opening the plugin UI in a DAW on Windows.

Workaround: after iplug_add_plugin(), manually add main.rc with RC
include paths to VST3 and CLAP targets. Tracked upstream fix as
rverse-1qa.

commit.message:
Fix async/await regression: route async.* host functions through call_host_fn

The call/apply/bind validation commit changed vm_call_function_value's
VmObject branch from call_host_fn to call_named_host_function_with_this.
However, call_named_host_function_with_this only recognizes specific
prefixes (promise., array., etc.) and falls through to
call_named_host_function for unrecognized names. The 'async.resume.fulfill'
and 'async.resume.reject' callbacks (used for await suspension/resumption)
start with 'async.' which was not in the prefix list, so they silently
dropped — causing await to never resume.

Add 'async.' to the prefix routing in call_named_host_function_with_this.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
